componentInstanceProperties.name: richTextValue
componentInstanceProperties.value:
Individual relationships allow you to connect individuals to each other. 


Individual affiliations are how you connect individuals to households and to organizations. These are most common to identify an individual's household, employer, or connection to other nonprofits.


Group affiliations are relationships between two groups, either households or organizations. An extended family can be connected together using this type of relationship. Another example would be a corporate headquarters and their branches, or a national nonprofit organization and their local organizations. In these two examples the group affiliation complements and adds additional detail to the Parent Account field.

componentInstanceProperties.name: richTextValue
componentInstanceProperties.value:
Individual relationships are recorded in the Contact Contact Relationship object and their roles are defined by Party Role Relationship records in your system. Below is a list of common Party Role Relationships that you can select and create.


Roles are intentionally created to be broad and inclusive. Roughly equivalent roles like Parent/Guardian and Spouse/Partner are paired together, because precise relationship details aren't always known when entering data. These roles are also gender neutral for similar reasons.


To create a role not displayed below, manually create one by going to the Party Role Relationship tab at the top of this screen and click “New.”

componentInstanceProperties.name: richTextValue
componentInstanceProperties.value: Group affiliations are recorded in the Account Account Relationship object and their roles are defined by Party Role Relationship records in your system. Below is a list of common Party Role Relationships that you can select and create. You can also manually add your own if you don’t find it below by going to the Party Role Relationship tab at the top of this screen and clicking “New.”

componentInstanceProperties.name: richTextValue
componentInstanceProperties.value:
Individual affiliations are recorded in the Account Contact Relationship object. Roles are defined by a multi-select picklist field. Navigate to that object in Object Manager to review and update the Role field according to your nonprofit’s needs.


A single individual can only have one individual affiliation (ACR record) with any organization or household. The role field should be used to indicate their current role or their highest-status role. If more detailed history of various roles over time is needed, an additional standard object like contact profile or a custom object is recommended.

componentInstanceProperties.name: richTextValue
componentInstanceProperties.value:
Review the flows below to understand what automation is included in this project for creating and managing relationships.


  • SMQS Screen Create Relationship or Affiliation — Screen flow for creating an individual relationship, individual affiliation, or group affiliation from a single guided entry point.
  • SMQS Contact Contact Relationship Post Save Flow — When a new individual relationship is created, automatically creates the inverse relationship record and links the two together.
  • SMQS Contact Contact Relationship Delete — Automatically deletes the inverse relationship record when an individual relationship is deleted.
  • SMQS Account Account Relationship Post Save Flow — When a new group affiliation is created, automatically creates the inverse affiliation record and links the two together.
  • SMQS Account Account Relationship Delete — Automatically deletes the inverse affiliation record when a group affiliation is deleted.
  • SMQS Party Role Relationship Before Save Flow — Completes fields on the inverse Party Role Relationship record when a new relationship is created.

componentInstanceProperties.name: label
componentInstanceProperties.value: 1b. Set Up Individual Relationships (Contact Contact Relationships)

componentInstanceProperties.name: label
componentInstanceProperties.value: 1c. Set Up Group Affiliations (Account Account Relationships)

componentInstanceProperties.name: label
componentInstanceProperties.value: 1d. Set Up Individual Affiliations (Account Contact Relationship)

componentInstanceProperties.name: richTextValue
componentInstanceProperties.value:
Stakeholder Management Quick Start

This page contains the necessary steps to set up Salesforce to manage the people and groups your nonprofit works with.

componentInstanceProperties.name: richTextValue
componentInstanceProperties.value:
1. Relationships and Affiliations

There are three broad types of relationships, individual relationships, group affiliations, and individual affiliations.

componentInstanceProperties.name: richTextValue
componentInstanceProperties.value:
Addresses, phone numbers, and email addresses are collectively referred to as contact points. These represent the various ways to reach the groups and individuals central to your mission. For groups (both organizations and households), the system supports tracking addresses and phone numbers; for individuals, you can track addresses, phone numbers, and email addresses.


For all stakeholders, you can track a limited number of these contact points directly on the Account record, with additional ones as Contact Point Address, Contact Point Phone, and Contact Point Email records.

componentInstanceProperties.name: richTextValue
componentInstanceProperties.value:
On these respective records those that have the ‘Primary’ checkbox marked, indicate the address, phone, or email used on the Account record. Additionally on these records you can indicate the quality of the information, to mark an address as ‘Undeliverable’ or to provide an ‘Invalid Reason’ for phones and emails. 


Finally for household members you can keep addresses in sync across all members of the household. On the individual affiliation (Account Contact Relationship) record you can mark how these addresses are synced. 


To get started, navigate to a group or individual Account page, then select the Addresses, Emails, or Phones tabs. You can edit the address, email, or phone fields directly or use the related list to ‘Mark Primary’ or ‘Undeliverable’. You can also edit the Account fields directly by marking the ‘Do Not Call’ or ‘Email Opt Out’ checkboxes.

componentInstanceProperties.name: richTextValue
componentInstanceProperties.value:
Review the flows below to understand what automation is included in this project for managing and syncing contact information.


Screen Flows

  • SMQS Screen Manage ContactPoints — Screen flow launched from an Account record for managing addresses, phones, and emails together for both individuals and organizations.
  • SMQS Screen RelatedList ContactPointEmail — Read-only screen flow on the Account record page that displays ContactPointEmail records in two tables: active/valid emails and inactive/invalid emails. Because ContactPointEmail is not enabled for dynamic related list single, this flow provides a consolidated view of all email records. Use Manage Contact Info to edit existing records or add secondary email addresses.
  • SMQS Screen RelatedList ContactPointPhone — Read-only screen flow on the Account record page that displays ContactPointPhone records in two tables: active/valid phones and inactive/invalid phones. Because ContactPointPhone is not enabled for dynamic related list single, this flow provides a consolidated view of all phone records. Use Manage Contact Info to edit existing records or add secondary phone numbers.

Address Sync

  • SMQS Address Sync AccountContactRelation Household — When a household member's sync setting is active, copies the household billing address to the member's mailing address.
  • SMQS Address Sync Business Account to ContactPointAddress — When a business account's billing address changes, syncs the update to its Contact Point Address records.
  • SMQS Address Sync ContactPointAddress to Account — When a primary Contact Point Address changes, syncs the address back to the parent account.
  • SMQS Address Sync Person Account to ContactPointAddress — When a person account's mailing address changes, syncs the update to its Contact Point Address records.
  • SMQS Address Sync Set ContactPointAddress Defaults — Sets default field values on new Contact Point Address records before they are saved.
  • SMQS Address Sync Upsert ContactPointAddress — Shared subflow called by the Business Account and Person Account address sync flows. Upserts a single ContactPointAddress record for a given Account and address slot.

Email Sync

  • SMQS Email Sync Account to ContactPointEmail — When an account's email field changes, creates or updates the corresponding Contact Point Email record.
  • SMQS Email Sync ContactPointEmail to Account — When a primary Contact Point Email changes, syncs the email value back to the parent account.

Phone Sync

  • SMQS Phone Sync AccountContactRelation to Household Account — When a household member's ACR is updated, syncs their phone to the household account.
  • SMQS Phone Sync Account to ContactPointPhone — When an account's phone field changes, creates or updates the corresponding Contact Point Phone record.
  • SMQS Phone Sync ContactPointPhone to Account — When a primary Contact Point Phone changes, syncs the phone value back to the parent account.
  • SMQS Phone Sync Household Account to Person Account — Syncs the household account's phone to all member person accounts.
  • SMQS Phone Sync Person Account to Household Account — Syncs a person account's phone change up to the household account.
  • SMQS Phone ContactPointPhone Helper Subflow — Shared subflow called by SMQS Phone Sync Account to ContactPointPhone once per phone field. Upserts the active ContactPointPhone record for the new phone value on a given Account.

componentInstanceProperties.name: richTextValue
componentInstanceProperties.value:
2. Contact Points

Addresses, phone numbers, and email addresses are collectively referred to as contact points.﻿

componentInstanceProperties.name: richTextValue
componentInstanceProperties.value:
Your nonprofit works with individuals, households, and organizations to carry out your mission. As an end user, you can track each of these stakeholders by creating Account records. 


Individuals are represented as Person Account records. We will use individuals to mean Person Accounts. A Person Account is both an Account record and also a Contact record, ensuring that anywhere in the system you need to reference an individual (as either an Account record or as a Contact record), you can do so easily.


Additionally groups are represented as Business Account records. Most nonprofits will use Business Accounts to represent organizations, and some will also use them to represent households. We will use groups to mean Business Accounts. The type of group is defined by a single Party Relationship Group record that serves as an extension to the Account record.

componentInstanceProperties.name: richTextValue
componentInstanceProperties.value: The purpose of households is to organize individuals that you are engaging together in a group, often by a shared mailing address but sometimes a close or extended family group. If your nonprofit only works with individuals one on one, meaning many of the individuals would be in single person households then you can and should disable the household features included in this quick start. You can choose to keep these features in your environment, to enable future activation or you remove them completely.

componentInstanceProperties.name: richTextValue
componentInstanceProperties.value:
To install the Stakeholder Management Quick Start app you need to create at least one Account record type for Business Accounts, and enable Person Accounts. Record types provide users the choice to create an individual or a group and have different fields shown to them, and different picklist values. The record type field is how the system identifies if an Account record represents an individual or a group. 


Go to Setup > Object Manager and select Person Account (not Account). Review record types. Repeat for the Account object. If you do not see Person Account in the list of objects after searching, you will need to turn on Person Accounts; go to Setup and search “person account” in the Quickfind, and follow the steps provided.


It is best practice to have the minimum number of record types possible across all objects, and for each record type to be distinct and exclusive. Meaning that an individual or group cannot possibly fall under more than one record type. We recommend two record types, one under Account for Groups, and one under Person Account for Individuals. Only add additional record types if there is a clear business need.


To get started, navigate to the Account tab and click ‘New’ on the top right corner. Select the record type of either group. To create a household, select Household and then check the ‘Is Household’ checkbox.

componentInstanceProperties.name: richTextValue
componentInstanceProperties.value:
Review the flows below to understand what automation is included in this project for groups, organizations, and households.


  • SMQS Screen Manage Household — Screen flow launched from an Account record for managing household members, addresses, phones, and emails, including which members will receive address syncs.
  • SMQS Person Account Conversion from Contact — Automatically converts a standard Contact record to a Person Account when triggered.
  • SMQS Household Setup IsHousehold Enforcement — Keeps the Household record type and the Is Household field in sync when household accounts are created or updated.
  • SMQS Household Setup Account to Party Relationship Group — Creates a Party Relationship Group record when a new household account is set up.
  • SMQS Household Setup Name Sync Account to CPA — When a household account name changes, updates the name on all related Contact Point Address records.
  • SMQS Household Setup Name Sync Account to PRG — When a household account name changes, mirrors the update to all related Party Relationship Group records.
  • SMQS Household Setup Name Sync AccountContactRelationship to Account — Updates the household account name when the primary household member changes.
  • SMQS Household Setup Name Sync ACR Delete to Account — Re-evaluates the household account name when a household member is removed.
  • SMQS Screen Manage Household Subflow Create Person Accounts — Subflow called by SMQS Screen Manage Household. Uses Transform to create Person Accounts and AccountContactRelations in a single transaction.

componentInstanceProperties.name: richTextValue
componentInstanceProperties.value:
To help with setting up contact points, relationships, and affiliations it is helpful to create a type of each record your nonprofit will use and to then view those record pages. 


To create an organization or individual, navigate to the Account tab and click ‘New’ on the top right corner.


To create a household, either repeat those same steps and choose ‘Household’ and then check the ‘Is Household’ checkbox.

FlexiPage.masterLabel: Stakeholder Management Quick Start Home Page Default
